title: Executive Order 10795—Designating the Intergovernmental Maritime Consultative Organization as a Public International Organization Entitled To Enjoy Certain Privileges, Exemptions, and Immunities

flags.justification: A narrow, technical designation under an existing statutory framework, implementing a Senate-ratified treaty.
A clear, specific designation tied to a named treaty and statute.
A designation under an existing statutory immunity framework, not a new compliance regime.
No compliance regime is created by this order.
A standing international-organization designation, evergreen by nature.
No existing oversight mechanism is removed; the order explicitly does not abridge any separately acquired privileges or immunities.
No compliance regime is created by this order.
A narrow, technical designation implementing an already-ratified treaty.
No second agency's competing mandate is implicated.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.

notes: Validation batch (negative). Structural weight: 0/10 = 0.0%. Designates the predecessor organization to today's International Maritime Organization.
